data.description:
## Why

`.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` already has the full key-materialize + job-level
`REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY` wiring (story 9e76/cc0b), but it is **secret-gated**: with the
`REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` secret unset it writes **unsigned** (advisory). Exactly one
CI-authored signed event exists store-wide. Flipping enforcement would turn every CI ticket
write red.

## What

Turn on real CI signing by populating the secret and confirming steady-state signed events —
the workflow wiring already exists, so this is provisioning + verification, not new plumbing:

- Populate the `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` GitHub secret with the Rebar Bot **private** key (the
  public key is already registered on the Rebar Bot identity).
- Confirm `reconcile-bridge` (and its canary) produce signed events on a real run.

## Scope

The GitHub Actions workflows that write tickets (`reconcile-bridge` + canary). Secret
population + verification only. No enforcement flip here.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` secret is populated with the Rebar Bot signing private key
      (public key registered on the Rebar Bot identity).
- [ ] A `reconcile-bridge` run writes at least one **signed** event authored by the bot
      identity; `rebar verify-identity` in CI reports it `verified` (exit 0).
- [ ] [operator-attested] the secret is set in GitHub settings — record the run URL and the
      resulting signed event id as proof on this ticket.
data.title: Populate REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY and confirm GitHub Actions produces signed ticket events
